review_notes: The functions accurately map to Kubernetes security controls relevant to customer data protection. They ensure network policies restrict traffic, services are not unnecessarily exposed, and secrets are encrypted at rest. The naming conventions are mostly followed, but minor improvements could enhance clarity.
naming_improvements: Consider renaming 'k8s_service_type_not_nodeport' to 'k8s_service_nodeport_disabled' for better clarity and consistency with naming patterns.
title: Customer Data Protection
step1_reasoning: The control 'Customer Data Protection' focuses on ensuring data is not publicly accessible and is protected. In Kubernetes, this can be mapped to ensuring that network policies are in place to restrict ingress and egress traffic, services are not exposed unnecessarily, and secrets are encrypted at rest. NetworkPolicies can be used to ensure that pods are not accessible from the internet by default. Similarly, ensuring that services are not of type NodePort can prevent unintended exposure. Encrypting secrets at rest aligns with protecting sensitive data.
